behaviors.description: SUM formula evaluates to correct sum of cell range
behaviors.justification: Pure in-process formula evaluation logic. Tests a single function (SUM) with controlled test data. No I/O, no inter-module dependencies, no mocking that could hide bugs. The real formula engine computes the sum, and we verify the result.
behaviors.test_description: Create cells A1:A10 with values 1,2,3,...,10. Create formula cell with =SUM(A1:A10). Evaluate and verify result equals 55. Test edge cases: empty range returns 0, single cell, negative numbers, floating-point values.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test executes the real SUM function and verifies output; would fail if SUM skips cells, adds wrong values, or mishandles edge cases.

behaviors.description: Cascaded formulas (B1=A1*2, C1=B1+1) produce correct result
behaviors.justification: Pure in-process formula evaluation with multiple cells in correct dependency order. Tests cell reference resolution and cascading evaluation within the formula engine. No I/O, no separate modules to mock.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=5. Create formula B1=A1*2 (evaluates to 10). Create formula C1=B1+1 (evaluates to 11). Evaluate C1 and verify result is 11. Test with negative numbers and floats. Verify that B1 is evaluated before C1 and intermediate result is correct.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test verifies the reference chain is resolved correctly and formulas are evaluated in correct order.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1→B1→A1) is detected and returns error
behaviors.justification: Tests the cycle detection algorithm in isolation. Pure algorithmic logic applied to a graph of cell references. No I/O or external dependencies. The algorithm must detect the cycle before evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Create cells where A1 contains =B1+1 and B1 contains =A1+1. Attempt to evaluate either cell. Verify that a circular reference error (e.g., #CIRC or similar) is returned instead of hanging, looping, or returning a value.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test would fail if cycle detection is missing or if evaluation hangs/crashes instead of returning an error.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact match returns correct value; missing key returns error
behaviors.justification: Tests a single built-in function with controlled test data. Pure in-process evaluation. No mocking of lookup logic; the test provides the lookup table and verifies the function's behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create lookup table with key-value pairs: {apple:1, banana:2, orange:3}. Test VLOOKUP(apple) returns 1. Test VLOOKUP(missing_key) returns error (not null, not wrong value). Test case sensitivity if applicable. Verify exact match is found correctly and closest-match logic is not used.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test would fail if VLOOKUP returns wrong value, misses exact matches, or returns wrong-value instead of error for missing keys.

behaviors.description: Nested IF conditions evaluate to correct branch
behaviors.justification: Tests nested function call evaluation logic. Pure in-process evaluation with test data. The formula engine must parse and evaluate nested conditions in correct order.
behaviors.test_description: Test formula =IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low"). With A1=5, expect "low". With A1=15, expect "medium". With A1=25, expect "high". Verify all three branches evaluate correctly.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test would fail if nesting is not parsed correctly or if wrong branch is evaluated.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE skips empty cells instead of inserting blanks
behaviors.justification: Tests a single function's special handling of empty cells. Pure in-process evaluation. The test verifies the function's specific behavior with edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Create cells A1="Hello", B1=(empty), C1="World". Formula =CONCATENATE(A1,B1,C1) should return "HelloWorld", not "Hello World". Test leading empty cells, trailing empty cells, and multiple consecutive empty cells. Verify empty cells are skipped, not converted to spaces or nulls.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test would fail if CONCATENATE inserts spaces/blanks for empty cells or does not skip them.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (Sheet2!A1) resolves and evaluates correctly
behaviors.justification: Tests multiple modules wired together: reference parser (parses Sheet2!A1), sheet resolver (looks up Sheet2), and formula evaluator. Mocking the sheet resolver would hide bugs in reference parsing or sheet lookup. Must verify the reference resolution system end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Create two sheets: Sheet1 and Sheet2. Set Sheet2!A1=10. In Sheet1, create formula =Sheet2!A1+5, evaluate, verify result is 15. Test error case: reference to non-existent sheet returns #REF! error. Test sheet names with spaces/special characters parse correctly.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test would fail if sheet reference parsing is broken, sheet lookup fails, or reference resolution is not properly integrated. Mocking the sheet getter would hide reference parsing bugs.

behaviors.description: When cell changes, dependents recalculate in topological order
behaviors.justification: Tests the integration of three systems: dependency tracking (which cells depend on which), change notification (when A1 changes), and recalculation engine (recalculate in correct order). Mocking any component would hide real bugs. Must verify correct propagation and order.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=1, B1=A1+1 (2), C1=B1+1 (3), D1=A1+B1 (3). Change A1 to 10. Verify B1→11, C1→12, D1→21. Verify topological order: A before B and D, B before C. Verify no cell recalculates twice. Verify all dependents are notified.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test would fail if recalculation is skipped, in wrong order, or if a dependent cell is missed. Pure mocking of any component would hide such bugs.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula and dependent values
behaviors.justification: Tests integration of three systems: undo/redo state management, formula restoration, and dependent cell recalculation. Must verify that undo restores both the edited formula text AND recalculates dependent cells to their previous values.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=5, B1=A1+1 (6). Edit B1 to =A1+2 (7). Undo. Verify B1 formula is restored to =A1+1 and value is 6. Change A1 to 10, verify B1 recalculates to 11 (using restored formula). Test multiple undos: make 3 edits, undo each one, verify correct state restored each time.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test would fail if undo doesn't restore formula text, doesn't recalculate dependents, or restores wrong state. Requires real undo/redo and recalculation systems integrated.

behaviors.description: Save/load workbook preserves formulas, values, and floating-point precision
behaviors.justification: Tests the binary serialization and deserialization layer. Requires real file I/O (not mocked). Mocking file operations would hide bugs in format encoding, data loss, precision truncation, or formula storage. Must use actual file I/O to catch real serialization bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook: A1=3.14159, B1=A1*2 (formula), C1="test", D1=A1+B1. Save to disk, load back. Verify values match exactly (test floating-point precision with assertEqual, not approximate). Verify formulas are stored as formulas, not pre-computed values. Verify relative vs absolute references work after load. Modify loaded workbook, save again, verify changes persist.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real file I/O to catch serialization bugs, precision loss, data corruption, or formula loss in the binary format.
